title: Kashmiri Rajma

ingredients.name: rajma (kidney beans), soaked overnight
visual_checks: use small Kashmiri rajma if available; regular red kidney beans work too
ingredients.notes: use small Kashmiri rajma if available; regular red kidney beans work too

subs.name: canned kidney beans (2 cans, 400 g each) — skip the pressure-cooking step
subs.notes: rinse canned beans well and add directly to the masala

ingredients.name: dried ginger powder (sonth)
visual_checks: this is in addition to the fresh paste and is key to Kashmiri character
ingredients.notes: this is in addition to the fresh paste and is key to Kashmiri character

subs.name: cumin seeds (1 tsp)
subs.notes: omit fennel and use cumin for a Punjabi-style rajma instead

subs.name: any neutral cooking oil
subs.notes: the pungent depth will be softer; add a small pinch extra of garam masala to compensate

steps.text: Drain the soaked rajma and pressure-cook with fresh water (covering by 5 cm) and salt for 4 to 5 whistles on medium flame. The beans should be completely tender but holding their shape. Drain and reserve the cooking liquid.
Heat mustard oil in a kadai over high flame until pale. Add the onions and cook on medium-high, stirring, until they turn deep golden, about 10 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook until the raw smell disappears, about 2 minutes. Add the tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook until they collapse and the oil separates, about 5 minutes.
Lower the flame and add Kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, ground fennel, dried ginger powder and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds to bloom the spices without burning them.
Add the cooked rajma and 250 ml of the reserved cooking liquid. Stir to combine, cover and simmer on medium for 15 minutes, letting the beans absorb the masala and the gravy thicken.

Taste for salt and sprinkle garam masala. Stir once and rest for 5 minutes. Finish with coriander leaves and serve with steamed rice or roti. Kashmiri rajma tastes even better the next day when the spices have deepened.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rajma → canned kidney beans (2 cans, 400 g each) — skip the pressure-cooking step (rinse canned beans well and add directly to the masala); mustard-oil → any neutral cooking oil (the pungent depth will be softer; add a small pinch extra of garam masala to compensate); fennel → cumin seeds (1 tsp) (omit fennel and use cumin for a Punjabi-style rajma instead)
